instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
response:
The team will commence the week with a collaborative brainstorming session to define the core requirements for the new app feature. Each developer must update their local repositories with the latest branch before initiating the coding phase. This initial alignment ensures that all members share a unified vision for the upcoming development cycle.

Monday through Wednesday will be dedicated primarily to writing clean and efficient code while adhering to established standards. The team should consult [Software Development Best Practices](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/software-development) to maintain high-quality deliverables throughout the sprint. On Thursday, the focus shifts to rigorous testing and bug fixing to ensure stability before the final review. Friday is reserved for documentation and preparing a comprehensive report for stakeholders. This structured approach guarantees that every task receives the necessary attention without compromising the overall project timeline.

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
